item.title: Security guard rescued from rubble 8 days after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Hernan Gil was brought out on a stretcher as elated rescuers cheered and hugged each other. He was loaded into a waiting ambulance and driven away.

item.title: Olympian David Hearn charged with destruction of property at Reflecting Pool
item.description: David Hearn is accused of ripping out a piece of sealant on the bottom of the Reflecting Pool on June 19, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said.

item.title: Maps show this July 4th could be hottest ever in parts of U.S.
item.description: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

item.title: Former Olympian indicted for allegedly tearing up part of Reflecting Pool
item.description: Former U.S. Olympian David Hearn was indicted on Thursday on a felony charge for allegedly tearing up roughly two feet of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool last month, amid the algae blooms and political mudslinging around the president's $16 million renovation. Nicole Sganga reports.

item.title: Couple who scaled Empire State Building facing multiple criminal charges
item.description: The two daredevils who climbed the spire of the Empire State Building in New York on Wednesday are facing a litany of criminal charges. According to prosecutors, the man told police he just wanted to "do something special" for his engagement. But now, Angela Nikolau says police have taken her ring as evidence. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: Heat wave threatens to melt some 4th of July plans
item.description: A heat wave is threatening to melt plans for America's 250th birthday, making parades and parties uncomfortable at best and downright dangerous at worst. At least 11 states are expecting temperatures above 100 degrees over the holiday weekend. Kris Van Cleave reports and Rob Marciano has the forecast.

item.title: U.S.-Iran talks pause for ayatollah's funeral after claims of progress
item.description: Negotiations between Iran and the U.S. will pause for a week for the funeral of Iran's slain supreme leader, but President Trump, and mediators, say there's been progress.

item.title: Former CDC official: RFK Jr.'s response to measles "not based on science"
item.description: Dr. Debra Houry, who resigned in protest from the health agency, said the spread of misinformation impacted lives.

item.title: U.S. military trailblazers commemorate America's bicentennial in 1976
item.description: CBS produced 912 "Bicentennial Minutes" segments over two and a half years to celebrate the U.S. bicentennial. CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett introduces two that feature prominent and path-breaking members of the U.S. military.

item.title: Chef Susan Bae shows off her dessert creations
item.description: Susan Bae, the pastry chef and partner at Moon Rabbit, a Vietnamese restaurant in Washington, D.C., has won the Outstanding Pastry Chef Medal at the 2026 James Beard Awards. Bae joins "The Takeout" with her culinary creations.

item.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

item.title: Balogun suspended after controversial World Cup red card
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Team won its first World Cup knockout match in more than two decades on Wednesday, beating Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-0. There was also controversy as Folarin Balogun picked up a red card, which will suspend him from the Americans' next match against Belgium. World Cup and Major League Soccer match analyst Brian Dunseth joins to unpack the team's performance.

item.title: New questions about Trump's taxes after financial disclosure release
item.description: President Trump's financial disclosure is raising many questions. For some, these include ethical concerns about whether he is profiting from the presidency. It's also highlighting another mystery: how much is he paying in taxes? CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has more.

item.title: AOC endorses Abdul El-Sayed in Michigan Senate race
item.description: Michigan progressive Senate candidate Abdul El-Sayed has scored an endorsement from Democratic Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez of New York, her first in a contested Senate primary for the 2026 cycle. Political strategists Joel Payne and Sarah Chamberlain join with analysis.

item.title: Former CDC chief medical officer Debra Houry discusses her resignation over RFK Jr.'s policies
item.description: In a CBS News exclusive, "Face the Nation" moderator and chief foreign affairs correspondent Margaret Brennan spoke with former CDC chief medical officer Dr. Debra Houry, who resigned in protest after HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. fired then-CDC Director Susan Monarez. Brennan joins "The Takeout" to unpack the interview.

item.title: State of Russia-Ukraine war after massive attack on Kyiv
item.description: Russia has launched its largest attacks on Kyiv since the start of the war with Ukraine, killing at least 17. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean joins to assess the state of the war.

item.title: 4th of July expected to be hottest on record: Travel impacts, forecast
item.description: This July Fourth is expected to be the hottest ever for millions of Americans as more than half of the U.S. will be trapped under a heat dome through the holiday weekend. CBS News' Kris Van Cleave reports on how this may impact travel and Rob Marciano has the forecast.

item.title: Taxes on Trump's $1.4 billion crypto income could total hundreds of millions, sources say
item.description: President Trump made $1.4 billion through his crypto ventures in 2025, according to a new financial disclosure. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has reporting on the potential tax payments the president may have to make based on those profits.

item.title: America 250 celebrations in Washington, D.C., deemed national special security event
item.description: Washington, D.C., is bracing for the heat and ramping up security in advance of the nation's 250th birthday celebration on the National Mall. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga reports on the security efforts. Then, Amb. Monica Crowley, U.S. chief of protocol and administration representative to Freedom 250, joins to discuss the festivities.

item.title: McConnell still hospitalized after EMS responded to his home last month
item.description: A spokesperson said the Kentucky Republican "continues to improve."

item.title: What the Founding Fathers said about the struggle against King George III, British Empire in 1776
item.description: In celebration of America's 250th birthday, CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett offers a small sampling of thoughts from 1776 about the Revolutionary War.

item.title: Friend of couple who scaled Empire State Building: "They did nothing to endanger anyone"
item.description: The couple that scaled the Empire State Building made a court appearance on Thursday, facing charges of reckless endangerment, criminal mischief, burglary and base jumping. Anna Martinez, one of their close friends, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for July 5, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and former CDC Chief Medical Officer Dr. Debra Houry.

item.title: CBS to host "The Great American Block Party 250" special on July 4
item.description: CBS will host a primetime special celebrating America's 250th birthday on Saturday, July 4, with exclusive performances and the largest fireworks show in history.

item.title: Celeb planners on how MSG could become Taylor Swift's perfect wedding venue
item.description: Two elite event designers shared their thoughts on how Taylor Swift's team might transform Madison Square Garden.

item.title: Taxes on Trump's $1.4 billion crypto income could total hundreds of millions
item.description: The White House declined to say how much President Trump paid in taxes on his crypto windfall.

item.title: StubHub accused of failing to deliver pricey World Cup tickets
item.description: Ticket reseller StubHub abruptly canceled customers' tickets to World Cup matches, costing them thousands of dollars, a lawsuit alleges.

item.title: Why tick season is expected to be worse than normal
item.description: The CDC is reporting the highest rate of emergency room visits from tick bites since 2017 in many parts of the U.S.

item.title: Who climbed the Empire State Building? Here's what we know.
item.description: The two people taken into custody for climbing New York City's Empire State Building where they apparently got engaged have been identified as Ivan Kuznetsov, 32, and Angela Nikolau, 33.

item.title: Taylor Swift's history with MSG, from talent competition to wedding bash
item.description: Taylor Swift has had a decadeslong history with Madison Square Garden, performing there as early as 2003 and even celebrating her 30th birthday at the venue.
